title: maesa fraseriana
intro: Maesa fraseriana, also known as the Fraser's boxwood or mountain boxwood, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Maesa fraseriana include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and support immune function. Maesa fraseriana is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, and its leaves and roots are commonly used in traditional medicine.
